✓ Both value pins green; RED commit precedes seed commit; npm run build clean: git log shows test commit 49d5858 (tests only, +7 lines in config.test.ts) precedes seed commit a116eb4 (config.ts change); base config.ts lacked grok keys so pins were necessarily red at 49d5858; npm run build exited clean; full npm test green includes both pin tests.
✓ Overlay carries no grok lines; loadConfig resolves both grok tiers from DEFAULT_CONFIG; setup/taskTimeoutMinutes intact; full npm test green: grep for 'grok' in .tickmarkr/config.yaml returns nothing (exit 1); overlay still has setup: npm install --no-audit --no-fund and taskTimeoutMinutes: 15; the loadConfig test asserts both grok tiers plus those two overlay values against the real repo overlay and the full suite passed (TEST_OK).
✓ DEFAULT_CONFIG seeds grok-4.5-xhigh:mid and grok-4.5-fast-xhigh:cheap under cursor-agent with benchmark provenance: Diff adds both models to tiers["cursor-agent"].models with a provenance comment citing AA Intelligence 54, Terminal-Bench 2.1 83.3%, SWE-bench Pro 64.7%, research/promotion dates, and the no-benchmark→cheap rationale for the fast variant.
✓ .tickmarkr/config.yaml no longer duplicates the grok seeds (no drift): Overlay contains no grok- lines, and the diff adds a guard test that reddens if grok- lines reappear in the repo overlay (skipping gracefully when the gitignored file is absent).
✓ Value-pin test demonstrably RED before the seed landed (falsification discipline, DECISIONS #92): Commit 49d5858 adds only the pin test while src/config/config.ts at that point (per the base side of the diff) had models: { "composer-2.5": "mid" } — the pinned grok keys were undefined, so the assertions failed; the seed lands in the subsequent commit a116eb4.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): requested changes
- tests/config/config.test.ts 'loadConfig resolves grok tiers from DEFAULT_CONFIG after overlay dedup' asserts cfg.setup === 'npm install --no-audit --no-fund' and cfg.taskTimeoutMinutes === 15 — both come from the gitignored .tickmarkr/config.yaml overlay (DEFAULT_CONFIG has taskTimeoutMinutes: 30 and no setup). On a fresh clone/CI where the overlay is absent, npm test goes red. The sibling drill test guards with existsSync and even comments 'gitignored — absent on fresh clones/CI', so the author knew the overlay can be missing but left this test unguarded. Fix: guard the overlay-derived assertions behind the same existsSync check (grok tier assertions can stay unconditional since they resolve from DEFAULT_CONFIG).

Oracle (a) asserts Object.is byte-identity on the default path. ConfigError test present for non-numeric halfLifeRuns plus a ≤0 guard. No package.json change ⇒ zero new runtime deps. Preview-thread residual explicitly owned by 30-03 per the criterion itself.
✓ Resolution comment at the avail term citing the real DECISIONS.md entry; grep pin green and red-capable; pre-existing ROUTE-12 tests green with zero behavior diff: The ROUTE-16 comment sits directly above the avail term and cites .overseer/DECISIONS.md 2026-07-11 — verified that dated entries covering the ROUTE-16 penalty-only confirmation exist in that file. Default paths fall back via `??` to the unchanged module constants, and the full suite (including all pre-existing ROUTE-12 pins) is green, proving zero behavior diff.

Both are trailing optional opts params — pure, no module-state mutation.

✓ An override demonstrably changes a warm cell's score (plumbing is not inert): Oracle (b): halfLifeRuns:1 shifts n/qSum 2→1.5 (exact pins, not just inequality), and availWeight:0.5 shifts a warm-throttled score from −AVAIL_WEIGHT to −0.5, with explicit not-equal-to-default assertions.
✓ zod fails closed on a non-numeric learnedTuning override: z.number().positive()/nonnegative() in the schema; test loads an overlay with `halfLifeRuns: fast` and asserts ConfigError; suite green.
✗ The ROUTE-12 penalty-only fork is resolved on the record: comment pin in profile.ts at the availability term, grep-pin oracle guards it — and SUMMARY records the fork resolution: The comment pin and grep-pin oracle are present and green, but no SUMMARY artifact recording the fork resolution exists in the diff or the worktree — .planning/phases/30-config-report-currency/ contains no 30-02 SUMMARY (only Phase 29 SUMMARYs exist).
